title: Balatonlelle

infobox.key: [MP](/wiki/List_of_members_of_the_National_Assembly_of_Hungary_(2018%E2%80%9322) "List of members of the National Assembly of Hungary (2018–22)")

infobox.value: * [Balatonboglár](/wiki/Balatonbogl%C3%A1r "Balatonboglár") * Balatonlelle * [Fonyód](/wiki/Fony%C3%B3d "Fonyód") (district seat) * [Lengyeltóti](/wiki/Lengyelt%C3%B3ti "Lengyeltóti") | [Coat of arms - Fonyód](/wiki/File:HUN_Fony%C3%B3d_C%C3%ADmer.svg "Coat of arms - Fonyód")

is a resort town located in [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") on the southern shore of [Lake Balaton](/wiki/Lake_Balaton "Lake Balaton"), about 35 km west of [Siófok](/wiki/Si%C3%B3fok "Siófok"). During the summer, the town becomes a holiday destination for many Hungarian and German families, and is known for its tourist attractions, including a 3 km long beach, an aqua park, a go-cart course, an annual wine festival, a numerous bars and restaurants. It is accessible from Budapest by train, taking approximately 2.5 hours.

**Balatonlelle** is a resort town located in [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") on the southern shore of [Lake Balaton](/wiki/Lake_Balaton "Lake Balaton"), about 35 km west of [Siófok](/wiki/Si%C3%B3fok "Siófok"). During the summer, the town becomes a holiday destination for many Hungarian and German families, and is known for its tourist attractions, including a 3 km long beach, an aqua park, a go-cart course, an annual wine festival, a numerous bars and restaurants. It is accessible from Budapest by train, taking approximately 2.5 hours.

Since the settlement forms part of the [Balatonboglár wine region](/wiki/Balatonbogl%C3%A1r_wine_region "Balatonboglár wine region"), a wine festival is often held in the first week of August.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonlelle&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In 1211, the area around Balatonlelle came under the jurisdiction of the [Tihany Abbey](/wiki/Tihany_Abbey "Tihany Abbey"). The first written mention of a settlement here from a 1229 letter, referring to the village as "*Lela*". In 1550, the town fell under [Turkish](/wiki/Ottoman_Empire "Ottoman Empire") control. Its population fell dramatically under Turkish occupation, and Turkish tax documents only record 8 families living here by 1580.

In the 18th century, the land was controlled by Hungarian [noble](/wiki/Nobility "Nobility") families; first the Majthényi family, and later the Szalay family. The town's first school was opened in the 1720s, however, notes from a 1778 [canonical visitation](/wiki/Canonical_visitation "Canonical visitation") reveal that this first school burned down not long after it was built.

In 1848, the town was elevated to [market town](/wiki/Market_town "Market town") status, and in 1864 it gained its current name of "Balatonlelle". In 1895, a two-classroom schoolhouse was constructed, and during the short-lived 1919 [Hungarian Soviet Republic](/wiki/Hungarian_Soviet_Republic "Hungarian Soviet Republic"), a third room was added to the school. A public elementary school was built in the town between 1941 and 1942.

Between 1979 and 1991 Balatonlelle formed a single settlement together with [Balatonboglár](/wiki/Balatonbogl%C3%A1r "Balatonboglár") under the name [Boglárlelle](/wiki/Bogl%C3%A1rlelle "Boglárlelle").

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Balatonlelle&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Demographics")]

As of 2023, the town's total population was 5018. As of the 2022 national census, the town was 87.3% [Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarians "Hungarians"), 2.1% [German](/wiki/Germans_of_Hungary "Germans of Hungary"), and 2.1% of non-European origin. The population was 38.9% [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church"), and 5.8% [Reformed](/wiki/Reformed_Christianity "Reformed Christianity").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
